title: Knowledge Bank
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Intelligent mollusk}}
{{Update|[[Strongholds of Resistance]]}}

}}
{{Quote|The Bank will devour your mind no matter what!|Local on Mon Calamari|<ref name="Underworld">''[[Star Wars: Underworld: The Yavin Vassilika]]''</ref>}}
The '''Knowledge Bank''', also known as '''Pakoukou''', was a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] native to [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] that was aware of all events that happened on the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] world. Consisting of a cluster of [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusks]], the Knowledge Bank used [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] to gain information on events all across the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] as well as from the minds of those who visited it. It would then relay that information to those who could communicate with it, either telepathically or with the special ritual [[Language/Legends|language]] other denizens of Mon Calamari used.

The Bank was sacred to the native [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]], and its existence was kept a closely guarded secret. A group of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] accessed it in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] in their search for the treasure known as the [[Yavin Vassilika]], and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cilghal]] used it in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] to find the missing [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|In appearance, the Knowledge Bank doesn't look all that impressive…|Leia Organa Solo|<ref name="JASB" />}}
[[File:Bank_Shelf.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The Knowledge Bank on its coral shelf]]
The Knowledge Bank was composed of a colony of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] bivalve<ref name="Fact File" /> [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusks]] who inhabited the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. The Bank had existed for millennia, though whether it was the same individuals or whether they had reproduced is unknown.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> The Knowledge Bank was highly intelligent,<ref name="Fact File" /> though because the intelligence was so different from other sentient beings, some observers gained the impression that it was not intelligent at all, but rather a passive recorder and transmitter.<ref name="JASB" />

The shells of Knowledge Bank mollusks were about a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] wide and glowed faintly. The mollusks opened with a groaning noise. Their interiors were soft and fleshy and had the appearance of a large, pulsing [[Brain/Legends|brain]]. They shone with a strong [[Color/Legends|golden]] light reminiscent of sunlight. Once open, the mollusks emitted a slow pulsing sound through the [[Water/Legends|water]].<ref name="Dark Apprentice" />

The Bank used [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] to store information in its database and communicate it to those who sought their knowledge.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> A network of non-sentient [[Fish/Legends|fish]]<ref name="Dark Apprentice" /> scattered throughout the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of Mon Calamari collected information and relayed it to the Bank.<ref name="JASB" /> The Bank mollusks themselves had incredible data storage abilities, allowing them to record the details of every event that had taken place in the waters of Mon Calamari.<ref name="Fact File" /> However, the Bank only held information on events within the Mon Calamari ocean,<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> as well as any information in the thoughts of those who had come near it.<ref name="Underworld" /> Furthermore, the information could become distorted over [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] theorized that the Bank's abilities were due to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], though she was unable to detect any Force usage from the organisms.<ref name="JASB" />

==Role on Mon Calamari==
The mollusks of the Knowledge Bank sat in a scattered jumble on a coral shelf twelve meters below the surface, in a narrow canyon near the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Coral Depths City|Coral Depths]].<ref name="Dark Apprentice" /><ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" />

Only a handful of Mon Calamari's inhabitants knew of the existence of the Knowledge Bank, most of them leaders and diplomats. They were also taught how to communicate with the organism.<ref name="JASB" /> Even fewer, mostly high-ranking leaders and priests of Mon Calamari, knew that the Bank was a communal intelligence.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> Leia Organa Solo believed herself to be the only [[Human/Legends|Human]] to know of the Bank's existence,<ref name="JASB" /> although at least two others, [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and her own [[Marriage/Legends|husband]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], had visited it too. However, there were others who knew of it as well. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], a local on Mon Calamari was known to rant to customers of his boats that the Bank was dangerous.<ref name="Underworld" /> Despite that, it was still a very well-kept secret, and many natives of Mon Calamari would have given their lives to prevent the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] from learning of it.<ref name="JASB" />

The [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] considered the Knowledge Bank the most precious resource of their world, and a priceless record of history.<ref name="Fact File" /> They treated it as a sacred object, and therefore refrained from performing any studies on it. To be allowed to visit it was an honor granted to few.<ref name="JASB" />

==Usage==
{{Quote|We have questions. We require access to the knowledge stored here in the great collection of memories. We must know if you have the answers we seek.|Cilghal, to the Knowledge Bank|<ref name="Dark Apprentice" />}}
Those who wished to communicate with the Knowledge Bank first needed to be in open water.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> Telepathic thoughts from the Bank could be heard as far away as the surface, at least twelve meters away.<ref name="Underworld" />

[[File:Knowledge-Bank.png|thumb|right|200px|Cilghal and Leia Organa Solo visit the Knowledge Bank.]]

The inhabitants of Mon Calamari communicated with the Knowledge Bank in a highly ritualized way. They would first address the Bank, asking it to give them the knowledge they were looking for. One of the shells would then open, revealing its swirling brain-like interior, and would emit a stream of yellow light.<ref name="Dark Apprentice" /> If the Bank chose to accept the question, several other shells would open and begin processing the information.<ref name="JASB" /> A long communion with the Bank would then begin,<ref name="Dark Apprentice" /> during which visitors had to use a special, slow, rhythmic language.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> There were also several ritualistic motions and sounds made in this part of the rite.<ref name="JASB" /> The Bank would finally telepathically transmit images and sounds directly into the mind of the questioner to answer their query.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" />

It was also possible to communicate with the Bank telepathically through the Force, without the use of the Mon Calamari rituals. That grew more difficult when more remote or specific memories were needed to be retrieved.<ref name="Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds" /> Some individuals were even known to access the knowledge of the Bank without the use of ritual or the Force. If the shells were open and broadcasting telepathically, they could sift through the millions of voices to find the one of the person whose information they sought.<ref name="Underworld" />

Use of the Bank by weak-minded individuals could be highly dangerous.<ref name="Fact File" /> Beings caught unaware could find their minds overwhelmed by the rush of thoughts. Some believed the Bank to be a dangerous organism that devoured minds and souls.<ref name="Underworld" />

==History==
[[File:Intelligent_Mollusc.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Tikkes's intelligent mollusk]]
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], [[Tikkes/Legends|Tikkes]] kept an intelligent mollusk, presumably a member of the Knowledge Bank, in a pool in his [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] office for record-keeping.<ref name="IWSWEI">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Episode I]]''</ref>

In 1 BBY, [[Illyan Webble]] came to Mon Calamari while on the run from a group of [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]]. When his skiff sank near the Knowledge Bank, Webble was eaten by Mon Calamari's native sea life, but his thoughts were stored within the Bank. A group consisting of Han Solo, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], Calrissian, [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]], and [[Jozzel Moffet]] later came to Mon Calamari in search of Webble, who knew the location of the treasured [[Yavin Vassilika]]. The local who rented them their skiff warned them of the Bank, insisting that it would devour their minds and take their souls. Ignoring them, they went in search of it anyway and eventually heard distant voices coming from the water below. Han, Lando, and Jozzel dove into the water, where they soon found Webble's sunken skiff and partially eaten body. They continued to follow the voices to their source, arriving at the Knowledge Bank. The trio listened to the millions of voices surrounding them, attempting to pick out Webble's voice. Jozzel eventually did, but was almost overwhelmed by the voices and had to be carried back to the surface.<ref name="Underworld" />

[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Bok (Mon Calamari)|Bok]] praised the Pakoukou after escaping the [[attack on Tak Base]].<ref name="SB">{{Tales|4|Sand Blasted}}</ref>

Over a decade later, in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], [[Cilghal]] and Organa Solo swam to the Bank in their search for [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], who had gone into seclusion after destroying the [[Cathedral of Winds/Legends|Cathedral of Winds]] on [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]]. Cilghal communicated with the organisms using the proper Mon Calamari rituals and soon learned where to find him.<ref name="Dark Apprentice" /> Leia would later reminisce about the event in her diary, though she believed herself to be the only Human who knew of the Bank's existence. Cilghal later seemed regretful that she had shown it to Leia.<ref name="JASB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Though most sources indicate that even the existence of the Knowledge Bank is a secret, some sources portray it as open information. ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'', for instance, states that Mon Calamari is famous for it. In addition, [[Star Wars: Underworld: The Yavin Vassilika|''Underworld: The Yavin Vassilika'']] has inhabitants of Mon Calamari freely speaking of it.

Furthermore, as indicated above by the different ways of accessing the Bank, ''Underworld'' gives a different picture of this process. The mollusks are already open and telepathically broadcasting any thoughts they have received. Nothing needs to be done to get them to open or communicate, and no special ritual or Force ability is needed to listen to them.

''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]'' established that Pakoukou,<ref name="SoR">''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]''</ref> which was originally referenced in "[[Sand Blasted]],"<ref name="SB" /> was another name for the Knowledge Bank.<ref name="SoR" />
